Rule [family:Modal]:
## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:
  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
  title -> <ModalHeader />
  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  actions -> map to appropriate child component (any)
  bodyAriaLabel -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  bodyAriaRole -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  footer -> ModalFooter (as children, ReactNode)
  hasNoBodyWrapper -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  header -> ModalHeader (as children, ReactNode)
  showClose -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  titleLabel -> map to appropriate child component (string)

Removed children (no longer valid JSX): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Add imports: ModalBody, ModalHeader, ModalFooter from '@patternfly/react-core'

Remove imports (if no longer used): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

### Fix 4
Lines: 118, 125, 171, 356
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ModalFooter

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

### Fix 5
Lines: 227, 250
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-descriptionlistterm]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <DescriptionListTerm> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <DescriptionListTerm> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <DescriptionListTerm variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></DescriptionListTerm>
  After:  <DescriptionListTerm variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <DescriptionListTerm> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <DescriptionListTerm><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</DescriptionListTerm>
  After:  <DescriptionListTerm icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</DescriptionListTerm>

Incident context:
  componentName: DescriptionListTerm
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: DescriptionListGroup

Component: DescriptionListTerm
Prop: icon

### Fix 6
Lines: 232, 260, 261
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-splititem-component]:
Component <SplitItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <SplitItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <SplitItem>{content} other children</SplitItem>
  After:  <SplitItem component=>other children</SplitItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: SplitItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Split

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: SplitItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

### Fix 7
Line: 18
Rule [sd-test-popover-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Popover is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Popover renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Popover may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Popover
  localName: Popover
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
   17    Button,
   18    Popover,
   19    Split,

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/details-page/DetailsPage.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix carefully:

**Fix 1 - Button.variant type change:** The `variant` values used are `"link"` (lines 173, 369) and `"secondary"` (mentioned in incident context). Looking at the code, only `"link"` is used, which is valid in both old and new types. The `"secondary"` mentioned at lines 119 and 126 doesn't seem to relate to Button usage — those are type definitions. The type change only adds `'stateful'` as a new option; no existing values were removed. **No change needed.**

**Fix 2 - Modal family migration:** Looking at the file, there is NO `Modal` component used or imported in this file. Lines 96-99 show `PageHeading`, not `Modal`. The `header` prop incident at line 96 and `showClose` at line 99 don't match — `PageHeading` is used, not `Modal`. **No change needed — no Modal in this file.**

**Fix 3 - useAccessReview type change:** The signature change is very minor (truncated in the description). Looking at the usage at line 301, it passes a `resourceAttributes` object. The type change appears to be backward compatible. **No change needed — usage is compatible.**

**Fix 4 - Icon children to prop on Button:** Lines 171-184 and 356-375 show `<Button>` containing both text content and a `PencilAltIcon`. Per the rule, when Button contains both icon AND text, move just the icon to the `icon` prop.

Let me look at each Button:

- Lines 171-184: `EditButton` component contains `{props.children}` followed by `<PencilAltIcon />` — icon should move to `icon` prop.
- Lines 356-375: Annotation button contains text and `<PencilAltIcon />` — icon should move to `icon` prop.

**Fix 5 - DescriptionListTerm icon prop:** Lines 227-229 and 250-252 show `<DescriptionListTerm>`. Neither contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons` as children — they contain text (`{label}`). **No change needed.**

**Fix 6 - SplitItem component prop:** The `component` prop on `SplitItem` in PF v6 is for specifying the HTML element to render (like `div`, `span`), NOT for receiving children content. The current usage is fine as-is. **No change needed — the `component` prop is an element type prop, not a content prop.**

**Fix 7 - Popover behavioral change:** This is informational about Popover's `appendTo` default changing. The code at line 234 uses `<Popover>` without specifying `appendTo`. This is a test-related advisory. No code change needed in the source file unless tests break. **No change needed in source file.**

Now let me re-examine Fix 4 more carefully:

Good. Now I have a clear picture. Let me reason through the applicable fixes:

**Fix 4 (Button icon children to prop):**

1. **EditButton** (lines 171-184): Contains `{props.children}` AND `<PencilAltIcon />`. This is icon + text — move the icon to the `icon` prop, keep text as children.

2. **Annotation Button** (lines 356-375): Contains text `{t('{{count}} annotation', ...)}` AND `<PencilAltIcon />`. Same pattern — move icon to `icon` prop, keep text as children.

All other fixes (1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7) are either already compatible, not applicable to this file, or informational only.

Let me apply Fix 4:

arguments.after:
<Button
      type="button"

      data-test={
        props.testId
          ? `${props.testId}-details-item__edit-button`
          : 'details-item__edit-button'
      }
      icon={<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />}
    >
      {props.children}
    </Button>
arguments.before:
<Button
      type="button"
      variant="link"
      isInline
      onClick={props.onClick}
      data-test={
        props.testId
          ? `${props.testId}-details-item__edit-button`
          : 'details-item__edit-button'
      }
    >

      <PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />
    </Button>

arguments.after:
<Button
              data-test="edit-annotations"
              type="button"
              isInline
              onClick={() =>
                launchModal(AnnotationsModal, {
                  extraProps: {
                    resource,
                    resourceModel,
                  },
                  isOpen: true,
                })
              }
              variant="link"
              icon={<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />}
            >
              {t('{{count}} annotation', {
                count: _.size(metadata.annotations),
              })}
            </Button>
arguments.before:
<Button
              data-test="edit-annotations"
              type="button"
              isInline
              onClick={() =>
                launchModal(AnnotationsModal, {
                  extraProps: {
                    resource,
                    resourceModel,
                  },
                  isOpen: true,
                })
              }
              variant="link"
            >
              {t('{{count}} annotation', {
                count: _.size(metadata.annotations),
              })}
              <PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />
            </Button>

content.text: Now let me verify the final file looks correct:
The file looks correct. Both `<Button>` instances have been updated to move `<PencilAltIcon>` from children to the `icon` prop. Let me verify there are no test files to check:

No test files exist. The changes are complete.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | Button.variant type change | **No change needed** | The type change only *adds* `'stateful'` as a new valid value. The values used in this file (`"link"`) remain valid. No code affected. Lines 119, 126 are type definitions unrelated to Button. |
| **Fix 2** | Modal family migration | **No change needed** | This file does not use or import `Modal`. The incident lines (96, 99) reference `PageHeading`, not `Modal`. False match. |
| **Fix 3** | useAccessReview type change | **No change needed** | The signature change is backward-compatible. The usage at line 301 passes a `resourceAttributes` object which matches both old and new signatures. |
| **Fix 4** | Button icon children to prop | **Applied** | Moved `<PencilAltIcon>` from children to the `icon` prop on two `<Button>` instances: (1) `EditButton` component (line 171–184) — icon + text pattern, icon moved to `icon` prop, `{props.children}` remains as children. (2) Annotations button (line 356–375) — icon + text pattern, icon moved to `icon` prop, annotation count text remains as children. |
| **Fix 5** | DescriptionListTerm icon prop | **No change needed** | Neither `<DescriptionListTerm>` instance contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons` as children. They contain text (`{label}`) — no icon migration needed. |
| **Fix 6** | SplitItem component prop | **No change needed** | The `component` prop on `SplitItem` is for specifying the HTML element type to render (e.g., `"div"`, `"span"`), not for receiving content. Current usage of `SplitItem` with children is correct as-is. |
| **Fix 7** | Popover behavioral change | **No change needed** | This is an informational advisory about Popover's `appendTo` default changing. No source code change required; would only affect tests (none exist for this file). |

### Fix 4
Lines: 118, 125, 171, 356
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-splititem-component]:
Component <SplitItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

  After:  <SplitItem component=>other children</SplitItem>

Rule [sd-test-popover-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Popover is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Popover renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Popover may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls
